message:
refactor(federation): decompose distributed supervision compiler into focused passes

Break the 180-line DistributedSupervisionCompiler::compile method into
discrete compilation passes, each responsible for a single concern:

Index construction:
- build_distributed_supervision_index: validates uniqueness, mailbox
  capacity, morphisms, and returns BTreeMap<String, &Spec>.

Node compilation:
- DistributedNodeCompilationPass: holds compiled nodes, mailbox routes,
  registry leases, and evidence hooks.
- DistributedNodeArtifacts: per-node compilation output bundle.
- compile_distributed_nodes: iterates the index and delegates to
  compile_distributed_node_artifacts for each node.
- compile_distributed_node_artifacts: constructs all four per-node
  artifacts (compiled node, mailbox route, registry lease, evidence
  hook) from a single spec.

Relation compilation:
- DistributedRelationCompilationPass: holds monitor plans, link plans,
  and failover handoffs.
- compile_distributed_relations: iterates nodes and delegates to
  per-relation helpers.
- extend_monitor_plans: builds DistributedMonitorPlan entries for
  each monitor target.
- extend_link_plans: builds DistributedLinkPlan entries with canonical
  pair deduplication.
- extend_failover_handoffs: builds DistributedFailoverHandoffContract
  entries with failure-domain validation.

The top-level compile method is now 12 lines: build index, run both
passes, assemble the plan. No behavioral changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
